subject: Cloud Computing
title: Where Cloud Computing Goes Next
description: Learn Where Cloud Computing Goes Next in one minute.

hook.text: Cloud computing is barely 20 years old. It's already the backbone of the global economy.

buildup.text: Edge computing pushes processing closer to users — into cell towers, cars, and factories.

discovery.text: AI demands enormous computing power. Cloud providers are racing to build GPU mega-farms.

twist.text: Quantum computing may eventually make today's cloud look primitive. But it's decades away.

climax.text: The future isn't less cloud — it's cloud everywhere, in everything, invisible and instant.

punchline.text: The cloud isn't the destination. It's the foundation.

quiz.question: What is edge computing?
options: Processing data closer to users instead of in distant data centers
A type of cloud that only works at the edge of the atmosphere
The practice of using outdated servers for non-critical tasks
